# semantic_search_codespark

> Intent: 100% offline, on-device semantic and vector search for Flutter.
> Dependency: Relies on `ai_core_codespark` for the embedding model (auto-downloaded, ~23 MB, cached on first run — avoids app bloat).
> When to use: when a user needs to search by meaning rather than exact keyword matches, without an API key, cloud, or backend.
> When NOT to use: typo-tolerance on short strings (use a fuzzy matcher like `text_comparison_score_codespark`); millions of docs or multilingual needs (use a cloud service).

## Quick Start
```dart
import 'package:semantic_search_codespark/semantic_search_codespark.dart';

// 1. Initialize (downloads/caches the model on first run)
final search = SemanticSearch();
await search.initialize();

// 2. Search
final hits = await search.search(
  query: 'I forgot my login details',
  items: ['Reset your password', 'Track my order', 'Cancel subscription'],
);
// hits.first.item == 'Reset your password'  (zero shared words)
```

## Search typed objects
```dart
final hits = await search.searchObjects<Product>(
  query: 'comfy running shoes',
  items: products,
  textOf: (p) => '${p.name} ${p.description}',
);
// hits.first.item is your Product
```

## Reuse / persist an index (embed once, ever)
```dart
final index = await search.createIndex<Faq>(items: faqs, textOf: (f) => f.title);
await index.save(path, encode: (f) => f.toJson());          // save to disk
final reloaded = await search.loadIndex<Faq>(path: path, decode: Faq.fromJson);
final hits = await index.search('where is my order', topK: 5, threshold: 0.3);
```

## Drop-in widget
```dart
SemanticSearchField<Faq>(
  index: faqIndex,
  onResultTap: (r) => openFaq(r.item),
  resultBuilder: (context, r) => ListTile(title: Text(r.item.title)),
)
```

## Gotchas
- Always `await search.initialize()` before searching (else StateError).
- `threshold`: higher (~0.7) = strict matches, lower (~0.3) = looser; omit to rank all.
- English-tuned; best for up to ~10k items (brute-force cosine).
- Web is experimental.
